title: agropyron pectiniforme subsp. sabulosum
intro: Agropyron pectiniforme subsp. sabulosum, also known as sand bluegrass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of North America and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of sand bluegrass include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of sand bluegrass are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
